title.value: SurveyX: Academic Survey Automation via Large Language Models
title.evidence: SurveyX: Academic Survey Automation via Large Language Models

value: SurveyX significantly enhances the efficacy of survey composition through innovative reference retrieval and pre-processing methods.
Experimental results show that SurveyX outperforms existing automated survey generation systems in both content quality and citation quality.
SurveyX's structured approach closely aligns with human expert performance in academic survey generation.

research_background.value: The exponential growth of literature has made it increasingly challenging for researchers to comprehend technological evolution and developmental trajectories of specific subfields.
research_background.evidence: However, the exponential growth of literature has made it increasingly challenging for researchers attempting to comprehend the technological evolution and developmental trajectories of specific subfields from the ground up.

main_contributions.value: SurveyX proposes an innovative system that integrates online reference retrieval, a pre-processing method called AttributeTree, and a re-polishing process to enhance survey generation.
main_contributions.evidence: To address these deficiencies, we propose SurveyX, an efficient and well organized system for automated survey generation.

core_approach.value: SurveyX decomposes the survey composing process into two phases: Preparation and Generation, utilizing innovative retrieval and processing techniques.
core_approach.evidence: SurveyX divides the composing process of surveys into two phases: the Preparation Phase and the Generation Phase.

reference_acquisition.value: References are acquired through a combination of offline sources from arXiv and an online crawler system based on Google Scholar.
reference_acquisition.evidence: The offline data source consists of 2,632,189 available papers downloaded from the arXiv.org, with new papers added daily. The online data source is a self-developed crawler system based on Google Scholar.

retrieval_algorithms.value: The retrieval algorithm includes a Keyword Expansion Algorithm for reference recall and a two-step filtering process for relevance.
retrieval_algorithms.evidence: The retrieval algorithm is divided into two steps: reference recall and reference filter... To achieve (1), we design a method called the Keyword Expansion Algorithm.

reference_preprocessing.value: A method called AttributeTree is used to efficiently extract key information from references, significantly enhancing information density.
reference_preprocessing.evidence: Based on this observation, we design a reference pre-processing method called AttributeTree.

outline_generation.value: Outline optimization is performed by generating hints from the attribute tree to guide the creation of a structured outline.
outline_generation.evidence: Inspired by this, we designed an outline generation method named Outline Optimization.

content_generation.value: Content generation utilizes hints derived from the secondary outline to guide the creation of the main body content, ensuring consistency.
content_generation.evidence: During the content generation stage, inspired by the human writing process for surveys, we continue to use the hint-based method employed during the outline generation stage.

post_optimization.value: Post refinement includes a RAG-based rewriting module and a figure and table generation module to enhance content quality and presentation.
post_optimization.evidence: We have constructed a comprehensive and systematic post refinement stage that significantly enhances the overall quality of the generated survey while expanding its expressive format.
